question.label: Opportunity ask
question.name: What opportunities have you wanted but not named out loud?
question.description: Drills into the specific opportunities he has not been explicit about so aspiration becomes concrete.

answer: To grow, I need to own a strategic bet with visible stakeholders.

assessment.note: He names a concrete aspiration — owning a strategic bet with visible stakeholders — which is a strong positive growth signal; no wellbeing signal was given.

new_queue.label: Strategic bet gap
new_queue.name: What stands between you and owning a strategic bet today?

new_queue.label: Executor mode
new_queue.name: Where are you still operating more like an executor than a strategic owner?

new_queue.label: Next move
new_queue.name: What would your next move look like before we next meet?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 6)

history.answer_excerpt: When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.
I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
To grow, I need to own a strategic bet with visible stakeholders.
